title: Choppin’ It Up: TGI Friday CEO’s Plans for Top-Line Growth
summary: TGI Friday’s is eliminating busy work from their general manager’s plates, allowing them to focus on the team members, Sugarloaf Holdings Founder and CEO Ray Blanchette tells Bloomberg Intelligence. In this episode of the Choppin’ It Up podcast, Blanchette sits down with BI’s senior restaurant and foodservice analyst Michael Halen to discuss TGI Friday’s focus on four-wall execution, hospitality and food quality to boost US same-store sales. Blanchette also comments on value, international store

title: Vanguards of Healthcare: SR One’s George on Next Biotech Cycle
summary: “I think this is an incredible time to be investing in biotech,” says Dr. Simeon George, founder and CEO of SR One, who joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Sam Fazeli to discuss why biotech’s next cycle will be global, selective and unforgiving. George traces SR One’s evolution from one of pharma’s oldest corporate venture arms to an independent firm with more than $2 billion raised. The conversation explores why AI’s real test is cutting development time and attrition, not just discovering tar

title: Mexico’s Energy Future Sits Between Two Superpowers (Podcast)
summary: Mexico is entering a pivotal period for its energy system. A new wave of reforms is opening the door to greater private investment in clean power, storage and grid infrastructure after years of subdued activity, creating one of the region’s largest energy transition opportunities. The shift comes against a complex geopolitical backdrop. title: US Jobless Claims Decline to 208,000, Below Forecasts
summary: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week, suggesting the labor market remains stable.

title: Stocks Fall After Retail Sales Amid AI Trade Angst: Markets Wrap
summary: A renewed selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks on concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with the market extending losses after retail sales data.

title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
summary: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a decline in assets over the past three years.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil steadied after a three-day rally as the US carried out another round of airstrikes on Iran, following a series of shipping attacks by the Islamic Republic this week.

title: SMBC Bolsters Global Sales and Trading Push With Macro Hire
summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. hired Kunal Maini as international head of macro rates, bolstering efforts to expand its sales and trading franchise beyond its core Japanese market.

title: US Drivers Are Again Paying More Than $5 a Gallon for Diesel
summary: American drivers are once again paying more than $5 a gallon for diesel, with a fresh round of hostilities in the Middle East raising the specter of more war-induced inflation.

title: US Stock Futures Today: AtaiBeckley, GE Aerospace, UnitedHealth
summary: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.2% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York. The US intensified strikes against Iran, hitting an oil tanker near the country’s main export terminal for the first time since the restart of the blockade on the Islamic Republic’s ports.

title: Abbott Boosts 2026 Profit Guidance as Sales Across Business Rise
summary: Abbott Laboratories raised its 2026 profit guidance after a stronger-than-expected second quarter driven by improved performance across most of its business lines.

title: US Power Grid Faces Defining Test for AI Boom
summary: The operator of America’s biggest electricity network is entering an emergency process to plug a projected supply shortfall.

title: Retail Traders’ Rise Upends the Established Stock Market Order
summary: Retail traders have moved from the stock market’s margins to its center.

title: Utility Warns Blast at South Africa Water Plant to Hit Supply
summary: An explosion on power lines that feed a key water-pumping hub risks cutting supply to parts of South Africa’s commercial hub of Johannesburg and key platinum- and gold-mining towns to the city’s west, the continent’s biggest bulk-water supplier said.

title: Middle East Bond Spreads Hit 2022 High as Ceasefire Breaks Down
summary: Bond investors are demanding the most compensation since October 2022 to fund Middle Eastern governments as fighting flares again between the US and Iran.

title: Nickel Hits Three-Week High on Indonesia Supply Risks, Fed View
summary: Nickel jumped to a three-week high as expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes faded, while uncertainty over mining policy in top producer Indonesia clouded the supply outlook.

title: Gold Declines as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Fuel Inflation Fears
summary: Gold declined, erasing a moderate gain from the previous session, as escalating tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns that energy prices and inflation will stay high.

title: French Power Exports Jump 36% to Record on Nuclear Output Boost
summary: French power exports surged to a record in the first half of 2026 as a rebound in nuclear output and rising renewable generation left the country with a big surplus of low-cost electricity to sell across Europe.

title: Broker Alpaca Raises $435 Million, Explores Prime Broking Entry
summary: Brokerage and financial infrastructure startup Alpaca has raised $135 million in equity and $300 million in debt, as it looks to expand a business that helps investors trade across financial markets using artificial intelligence agents.

title: DCC Gets Sweetened Bid From KKR, ECP After Drawn-Out Talks
summary: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners have sweetened their bid for DCC Plc to more than £5.7 billion ($7.7 billion) and bought extra time to complete what’s become a drawn-out takeover process.

title: TSMC Says AI Boom Will Go Another Three Years, Defying Skeptics
summary: The chipmaker is sharply increasing its capital spending and sales projections for the year.

title: Partners Group Slumps as Popular Funds Weigh on Asset Growth
summary: Partners Group Holding AG shares slumped after the Swiss manager of private equity and credit funds said client redemptions from its popular evergreen vehicles weighed on growth in the first half.

title: Goldman’s Bell Sees More Gains for Europe’s AI Buildout Stocks
summary: Europe’s AI infrastructure shares have further to run as hyperscalers are expected to increase spending again next year, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s Sharon Bell.
